popularStory.headline: en.Wikipedia Charles Brandon, Duke of Suffolk
popularStory.description: Charles Brandon, 1st Duke of Suffolk, 1st Viscount Lisle, KG PC (c. 1484 – 22 August 1545) was an English military leader and courtier. Through his third wife, Mary Tudor, he was brother-in-law to Kin …

zh.lifeSummary: 當Charles Brandon 1st Duke of Suffolk於1484年4月出生在英國諾福爾可金也斯 琳時，他的父親Sir William Brandon28歲，母親Elizabeth Bruyn11481歲。他於1507年2月7日之前與Margaret Neville結婚。他去世於1545年8月24日在英國素瑞吉爾德福德享年61歲，埋葬在英國英格蘭波克夏溫莎女爵聖 格奧智也斯 查阿培爾。

nameMeanings.name: Brandon
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: habitational name from any of various places called Brandon, in Durham, Norfolk, Suffolk, Warwickshire, and elsewhere. Most are named with Old English brōm ‘broom, gorse’ + dūn ‘hill’. One in Lincolnshire, however, may be named after the Brant River, on which it stands; Ekwall derives the river name from Old English brant ‘steep’, presumably with reference to its steep banks. This surname was known in Ireland early enough to have a Gaelicized form de Breandún.
Irish (Kerry): Anglicized form of Gaelic Mac Breandáin ‘son of Breandán’, a rare Kerry surname from the Latinized form of the name of the local saint, Brendan (Brendanus from Bréanainn).
French: from the Old French oblique case of the personal name Brando, of ancient Germanic origin (see Brand 1).

variants.name: Brand
meanings: English, Scottish, North German, Scandinavian, Dutch, and French (mainly Alsace and Lorraine): from the ancient Germanic personal name Brando, Old Norse Brandr, a short form of various compound names containing the element brand ‘sword’ (a derivative of brinnan ‘to flash’), of which the best known is Hildebrand . There is placename evidence for Brant(a) as an Old English personal name; however, the Middle English personal name Brand was probably introduced to England from Old Norse.
German: variant of   Brandt   1 and, in North America, (also) an altered form of this.
Swedish and Danish: ornamental name or nickname from brand ‘fire’.

variants.name: Brandom
meanings: English and Jewish (Sephardic): variant of   Brandon  .

nameMeanings.name: Charles
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: From a Germanic word, karl, meaning ‘free man’, akin to Old English ceorl ‘man’. The name, Latin form Carolus, owed its popularity in medieval Europe to the Frankish leader Charlemagne ( ?742–814 ), who in 800 established himself as Holy Roman Emperor. His name (Latin Carolus Magnus) means ‘Charles the Great’. Carolus—or Karl, the German form—was a common name among Frankish leaders, including Charlemagne's grandfather Charles Martel ( 688–741 ). Charles is the French form. The name occurs occasionally in medieval Britain as Karolus or Carolus; it had a certain vogue in West Yorkshire from the 1400s, particularly among gentry families. The form Charles was chosen by Mary Queen of Scots ( 1542–87 ), who had been brought up in France, for her son, Charles James ( 1566–1625 ), who became King James VI of Scotland and, from 1603 , James I of England. His son and grandson both reigned as King Charles , and the name thus became established in the 17th century both in the Stuart royal house and among English and Scottish supporters of the Stuart monarchy. In the 18th century it was to some extent favoured, along with James , by Jacobites, supporters of the exiled Stuarts, opposed to the Hanoverian monarchy, especially in the Highlands of Scotland. In the 19th century the popularity of the name was further enhanced by romanticization of the story of ‘Bonnie Prince Charlie’, leader of the 1745 rebellion.

en.lifeSummary: When Charles Brandon 1st Duke of Suffolk was born in April 1484, in King's Lynn, Norfolk, England, his father, Sir William Brandon, was 28 and his mother, Elizabeth Bruyn, was 11481. He married Margaret Neville before 7 February 1507. He died on 24 August 1545, in Guildford, Surrey, England, at the age of 61, and was buried in St George's Chapel, Windsor, Berkshire, England, United Kingdom.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Charles Brandon 1st Duke of Suffolk est né en avril 1484, à King's Lynn, Norfolk, Angleterre, son père, Sir William Brandon, avait 28 ans et sa mère, Elizabeth Bruyn, avait 11481 ans. Il a épousé Margaret Neville avant 7 février 1507. Il est décédé le 24 août 1545, à Guildford, Surrey, Angleterre, à l’âge de 61 ans, et a été enterré à St George's Chapel, Windsor, Berkshire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni.

ko.lifeSummary: Charles Brandon 1st Duke of Suffolk(이)가 1484년 4월 영국 노르폴크 킨그'스 린에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Sir William Brandon(은)는 28세였고, 그의 어머니 Elizabeth Bruyn(은)는 11481세였다. 그는 1507년 2월 7일 이전 Margaret Neville(와)과 결혼했다. 그는 1545년 8월 24일 영국 수리 길드퍼드에서 61세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 잉글랜드 버크실르 윈드소르 스트 조그'스 차플에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Charles Brandon 1st Duke of Suffolk nasceu em abril de 1484, em King's Lynn, Norfolk, Inglaterra, seu pai, Sir William Brandon, tinha 28 anos e sua mãe, Elizabeth Bruyn, tinha 11481 anos. Ele casou-se com Margaret Neville antes 7 de fevereiro de 1507. Ele faleceu em 24 de agosto de 1545, em Guildford, Surrey, Inglaterra, com 61 anos, e foi sepultado em St George's Chapel, Windsor, Berkshire, Inglaterra, United Kingdom.

ru.St George's Chapel, windsor, Berkshire,: ст джеорге'с чапел, виндзор, биркшир, Англия, Великобритания
ru.lifeSummary: Когда Charles Brandon 1st Duke of Suffolk родился апрель 1484 г. в местности кинг'с линн, норфолк, Англия, его отцу по имени Sir William Brandon было 28 лет, а матери, которую звали Elizabeth Bruyn, 11481. Он женился на женщине по имени Margaret Neville перед 7 февраль 1507 г. Он умер 24 августа 1545 г. в местности гуаилдфорд, сурри, Англия в возрасте 61 год и был похоронен в местности ст джеорге'с чапел, виндзор, биркшир, Англия, Великобритания.

es.Bishops Lynn, Norfolk, England: King's Lynn, Norfolk, Inglaterra, Reino Unido
es.St George's Chapel, windsor, Berkshire,: St George's Chapel, Windsor, Berkshire, Inglaterra, Reino Unido
es.lifeSummary: Cuando Charles Brandon 1st Duke of Suffolk nació en abril de 1484, en King's Lynn, Norfolk, Inglaterra, su papá, Sir William Brandon, tenía 28 años y su mamá, Elizabeth Bruyn, tenía 11481 años. Se casó con Margaret Neville antes de 7 de febrero de 1507. Murió el 24 de agosto de 1545, en Guildford, Surrey, Inglaterra, a la edad de 61 años, y fue sepultado en St George's Chapel, Windsor, Berkshire, Inglaterra, Reino Unido.
